CSCE 735 Syllabus

Section 600 (42401)

Parallel Computing

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

Credit Hours:  3

Credit Hours:  3

Instructor Details

Vivek Sarin

Email:  sarin@tamu.edu

Office Location:  415 Peterson

Phone:  979-458-2214

Office Hours

MW 3:30-4:30pm, via Zoom  https://tamu.zoom.us/j/99726975773 ; or by

appointment

Preferred Contact Method

Email, include 735 in the subject

Catalog Description

Parallel Computing. (3-0). Credit 3. Overview of parallel computing technology and

programming methods; topics include multiprocessor architectures, programming

tools, parallel performance, parallel algorithms and applications of parallel

computing. Prerequisites: Graduate classification or approval of instructor.

Additional Course Details

This course provides a graduate level overview of parallel computing technology

and programming methods.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Graduate classification or approval of instructor.

Course Learning Outcomes

The main objective of this course is to provide a comprehensive overview of parallel

computing that includes algorithms, software, and architectures, and their interplay

when developing high performance solutions to challenging large scale problems.

Upon successful completion of the course, a student:

will be able to design parallel algorithms and develop software

implementations on parallel computers;

will understand the architecture of modern parallel computing systems;

will be able to analyze the performance of parallel implementations on

various parallel platforms.

Special Course Designation

None

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

implementations on parallel computers;

Special Course Designation

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Optional

None

Notes:

Instructor will provide lecture videos, copies of lecture slides, and links to resources

on the internet on the course web site on Canvas.

Grading Policy

Assignments and projects will be available on the course web site on Canvas and

submissions will have to be uploaded to Canvas.

Course grade will be assigned on the basis of quizzes (40%), assignments (30%), a

minor project (10%), and a major project (20%). The following scale will be used:

A=90-100%, B=80-89%, C=70-79%, D=60-69%, F=0-59%. Scores are rounded up to

the nearest integer.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late assignments and projects are accepted with 10% penalty for each day late up

to a maximum of 3 days (e.g., a score of 80 will be reduced to 72 when submitted

within 24 hours after the deadline; it will be reduced to 64 when submitted

between 24 and 48 hours after the deadline).

Late quizzes will not be accepted.

Week
